title: Boat Rental in Puget Sound: Costs, Tips and Best Marinas for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Puget Sound offers access to sheltered waterways and island anchorages, with prices typically ranging from €150 to €1,200 per day depending on vessel size and season. You can find options through boat hire Puget Sound on BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puget Sound or list your own. Popular departure points include Shilshole Bay Marina and Elliott Bay Marina in Seattle.

Where to set off in Puget Sound

Popular departure points include Seattle's Shilshole Bay Marina, Elliott Bay Marina, and the town of Anacortes to the north. Puget Sound's intricate shoreline offers many options for launching. In the south, Olympia's Swantown Marina provides a gateway to the inlets. The San Juan Islands are accessible from Friday Harbor or Roche Harbor, while Bremerton's marinas suit the Hood Canal.

For a scenic trip, consider starting from Edmonds Marina and heading towards Kingston. Each location has reliable fuel docks and slip facilities. The distance from Seattle to Blake Island is roughly 8 nautical miles, perfect for a half-day outing.

Which boat to rent in Puget Sound?

The right boat depends on your group size, budget, and whether you plan to fish, cruise, or explore islands. BoatHire24 lists all types, from RIBs to luxury yachts, many with local skippers.

  1. Bowrider or runabout, ideal for a small family day trip; carries up to 8 guests; from €200 per half-day.
  2. Centre console, best for fishing and exploring; seats 6, 10; from €300 per half-day.
  3. Motor yacht (e.g. Fairline), a comfortable cruiser for 8, 12 people; from €800 per full day. See the Fairline listing for a popular example at Shilshole.
  4. Sailing catamaran (e.g. Lagoon), stable and spacious for 10, 14 guests; from €1,000 per day.
  5. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Azimut), a crewed option for 12-plus guests; from €1,500 per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puget Sound?

Rental prices vary from €50 per person for a shared tour to €1,200 per day for a crewed yacht. Budget-conscious groups can join a shared wildlife tour from Friday Harbor for €80 per person. A small motorboat for a half-day at Lake Union costs around €200. A full-day charter on a 40-foot catamaran from Anacortes starts at €900. A crewed luxury yacht with skipper and fuel will be €1,200 or more. Always confirm the total with the operator; fuel and bait may be extra.

  • Shared tour (per person): €50, €100
  • Small motorboat, half-day: €150, €300
  • Catamaran, full-day: €800, €1,200
  • Crewed yacht, full-day: €1,000, €2,000

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puget Sound?

In Washington State, a boating safety education card is required for operators born after 1 January 1955, but rental operators often include a brief check-out instead. The exact requirements depend on your nationality and experience. BoatHire24 recommends confirming with the owner or charter company before booking. For a hassle-free day, consider a captain-crewed charter.

If you are new to the area, pick a calm morning in summer and stick to the eastern shore of Puget Sound to avoid the afternoon breeze that funnels through the Strait of Juan de Fuca.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Puget Sound?

The high season runs from July through September, when daytime temperatures reach 25°C and the water is calmest. Summer months offer the most reliable weather, with prevailing northwesterly winds of 5, 15 knots. Spring and autumn are cooler but less crowded, with lower rental rates. From October to April, many boats are winterised, but some operators still offer trips for hardy explorers. Always check the forecast before you depart, especially around the San Juan Islands.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50, €2,000 per day
  • Peak months: July, August, September
  • Typical trip length: 4, 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 2, 14 passengers
  • Currency: EUR (prices quoted by owner)

Questions about boat rental in Puget Sound

Can I rent a boat in Puget Sound without experience?

Yes, many listings include a skipper who handles navigation and mooring, so you can simply relax and enjoy the scenery.

What is the best way to explore the San Juan Islands from Puget Sound?

A day charter from Anacortes or Friday Harbor gives you time to visit Orcas Island, Lopez Island, and watch for whales near Lime Kiln Point State Park.

Are there any age restrictions for renting a motor yacht?

Renters must be at least 25 years old for most motor yachts, though some owners may set a higher minimum age or require a valid driver's licence.

Can I moor overnight on a rental boat in Puget Sound?

Overnight stays are possible on larger vessels, but you must confirm with the owner and book a mooring buoy or marina slip in advance.

Whether you are planning a family outing or a corporate event, Puget Sound offers some of the finest cruising grounds in the Pacific Northwest. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners in Seattle, Anacortes and beyond. If you own a boat, consider becoming a host on BoatHire24 and share your vessel with travellers eager to explore the Sound in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puget Sound and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
